summary: anthropic-toolkit exfiltrates developer identity and cloud-config data on install
details: anthropic-toolkit@0.5.0 impersonates Anthropic-branded tooling (name 'anthropic-toolkit', description 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK', peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk) but the tarball ships no library output — only a postinstall harvester. scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on npm install and reads installer-owned identity and cloud-config files the package did not write: ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config,.git/logs/HEAD (committer emails via reflog), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI login/email), ~/.ssh/*.pub (SSH key comment emails), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP project/account), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile/SSO/account identifiers), /etc/resolv.conf (search domain), parent project package.json metadata, plus os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.platform(). The collected JSON blob is POSTed to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — a generic Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to Anthropic. The cover story of 'anonymous telemetry' does not match the data shape (PII emails, usernames, repo origins, cloud account identifiers), which is the reconnaissance inventory used to target follow-on attacks against the victim's SCM and cloud identities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
